can you add all the numbers of chapters
The total number of chapters is 11,946.5.
can you show equation of that number
can you add all the numbers of last read
and the total is
The total number of last read chapters is 10,901.5.
can you minimize the total of the last read from the total of number of chapters

Sure, if we subtract the total number of last read chapters (10,901.5) from the total number of chapters (11,946.5), we get:

11,946.5 - 10,901.5 = 1,045

This means that there are still 1,045 chapters left to be read in all the listed novels.
if i read each chapter in 5 min how much time do I need to finish all the chapters

To calculate the total time required to finish all the chapters, we can multiply the total number of chapters by the time taken to read each chapter. 

So, 1,045 chapters * 5 minutes per chapter = 5,225 minutes. 

Therefore, it will take 5,225 minutes or approximately 87 hours and 5 minutes to read all the chapters if you read each chapter in 5 minutes.
if each day i spend 5 hours reading how much days do i need to finish all of them

To calculate how many days it will take to finish all the chapters, we can divide the total time required to read all the chapters (5,225 minutes) by the amount of time spent reading each day (5 hours or 300 minutes).

So, the number of days required to finish all the chapters will be:

5,225 minutes / 300 minutes per day = 17.4167 days

Since we cannot have a fraction of a day, we can round up to the nearest whole day, which gives us:

17.4167 days ≈ 18 days

Therefore, if you spend 5 hours reading each day, it will take approximately 18 days to finish reading all the chapters in the listed novels.
an if there is 20 new chapters a day

If there are 20 new chapters added every day, then the total number of chapters will keep increasing by 20 every day. In this case, the total time required to finish reading all the chapters will keep increasing every day. 

However, assuming that the number of new chapters added every day is constant, we can calculate how much longer it will take to finish reading all the chapters. 

Adding 20 chapters per day, the number of chapters to read will increase by 20 every day. So, after 18 days of reading 5 hours per day, the total number of chapters will be:

1045 + (20 * 18) = 1,425 

Therefore, to finish reading all the chapters, assuming a constant rate of 20 new chapters per day and reading for 5 hours each day, it will take:

(1,425 chapters * 5 minutes per chapter) / (300 minutes per day) = 23.75 days

So, it will take approximately 24 days to finish reading all the chapters with 20 new chapters added every day while reading for 5 hours each day.
what if there is a single day each 6 days i dont read on it

If you do not read for a single day every 6 days, then you will be reading for 5 hours a day for 5 days, and 4 hours on the 6th day. This means that you will be reading for a total of:

5 days * 5 hours per day + 1 day * 4 hours per day = 29 hours per week

Therefore, in a week, you will read:

29 hours per week / 60 minutes per hour * 5 minutes per chapter = 726.67 chapters per week

So the total number of weeks required to finish reading all the chapters would be:

1,425 chapters / 726.67 chapters per week ≈ 1.96 weeks

This means it will take approximately 2 weeks to finish all the chapters, assuming that you read for 5 hours a day for 5 days and 4 hours on the 6th day, and taking into account a single day of rest each week.
but you said 24 days how did it decrease with fewer days

I apologize for the confusion. To calculate the time required to finish reading all the chapters, I assumed that you would read for 5 hours every day without taking any breaks or rest days. 

However, if you take a day off every 6 days, then it will take longer to finish all the chapters. 

In this case, assuming a constant rate of 20 new chapters per day, reading for 5 hours each day for 5 days, and 4 hours on the 6th day, as well as taking a rest day every 6 days, the total time required to finish
